text.title: Onboarding Flows Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Onboarding Flows` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Activation event MUST be a single named event with clear criteria (e.g. 'first Aha message sent in shared channel'). 'User explores the product' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vague activation events make measurement impossible; the named event is the only thing the team can optimise against.

rule.statement: Each new user MUST be segmented at signup with ≤ 1 question (role / use-case / company size). Multi-question intake before first value is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Multi-question intake balloons drop-off; one-question segmentation captures most value with minimum friction.

rule.statement: The critical path from signup to activation MUST be ≤ 5 required steps. Optional steps don't count; required-step count above 5 must be cut.
rule.rationale: Each additional required step loses 10-20%; the 5-step cap is the empirical sweet spot for SaaS activation.

rule.statement: Each in-app onboarding step MUST be paired with a triggered email that fires only when user is stuck > 24h. Email blasts unrelated to in-app progress are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Un-paired emails cause re-engagement spam; triggered email pairing converts at 2-3x batch sends.

rule.statement: The spec MUST name a single human owner accountable for the onboarding flow + activation metric. Group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group-owned onboarding stalls; named owner can prioritise iteration without committee.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
